class.name: JustAMCPRuntime
class.brief_description: Engine singleton that hosts the JustAMCP runtime server.
class.description:
[JustAMCPRuntime] runs the local MCP endpoint and routes incoming commands to built-in tools, resources, prompts, or custom command callables. It is independent of the active [SceneTree], so it can keep serving editor automation while scenes are opened, closed, or changed. [codeblock] JustAMCPRuntime.port = 7777 JustAMCPRuntime.enabled = true
JustAMCPRuntime.register_custom_command("double", func(value): return value * 2 )
var result = JustAMCPRuntime.execute_command("run_custom_command", { "name": "double", "args": [21], }) print(result["result"]) [/codeblock]

method.name: execute_command
method.description: Executes a runtime command with [param params] and returns the command result dictionary. Commands include built-in runtime operations, tool dispatch, resources, prompts, and [code]run_custom_command[/code].

method.name: poll
method.description: Processes pending network activity for the runtime server, including new connections, inbound MCP payloads, and queued asynchronous work.

method.name: register_custom_command
method.description: Registers [param callable] under [param name] so MCP clients can invoke it with [code]execute_command("run_custom_command", {"name": name, "args": [...]})[/code]. Registering the same name replaces the previous callable.

method.name: unregister_custom_command
method.description: Removes the custom command registered as [param name]. Calls to [code]run_custom_command[/code] with that name will return an error after removal.

members.member: When [code]true[/code], the runtime server is active and can accept MCP traffic. Setting it to [code]false[/code] stops serving runtime requests.
TCP port used by the JustAMCP runtime server. Change this before enabling the runtime when a project needs a non-default port.

signal.name: client_connected
signal.description: Emitted when a new MCP client connects to the runtime server.

signal.name: client_disconnected
signal.description: Emitted when an MCP client disconnects or the runtime closes its connection.

signal.name: command_received
signal.description: Emitted after the runtime parses an incoming MCP command. The signal exposes the command name and decoded parameter dictionary before dispatch.
